article_title: Moonshot AI Open-Sources Kimi K2.6, a Trillion-Parameter Model That Runs 300-Agent Swarms for Hours
summary: Most claims verify cleanly against the four cited sources, but one specific numeric comparison attributed to VentureBeat is not present in any of the four snapshots and must be removed or re-sourced before publication.

findings.message: Fabricated attribution: SWE-Bench Pro competitor figures cited to VentureBeat are not in the VentureBeat article.
findings.details: The sentence 'VentureBeat reports the SWE-Bench Pro figure compares to 57.7 for GPT-5.4 at its high-compute setting, 53.4 for Claude Opus 4.6 at maximum effort, and 54.2 for Gemini 3.1 Pro in thinking mode' cites VentureBeat, but none of the numbers 57.7 / 53.4 / 54.2, nor the phrases 'high-compute setting', 'maximum effort', or 'thinking mode', appear in the VentureBeat snapshot (source-2.html). A full-text search of all four source snapshots (SCMP, SiliconANGLE, VentureBeat, The Decoder) returned zero hits for those three numbers in article prose and zero hits for those three qualifier phrases. The 57.7 / 53.4 / 54.2 comparison values therefore have no verifiable source in this submission.

content_preview.title: Moonshot AI Open-Sources Kimi K2.6, a Trillion-Parameter Model That Runs 300-Agent Swarms for Hours
content_preview.summary: Moonshot released Kimi K2.6 under a modified MIT license, claiming parity with GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks while orchestrating agent swarms that run for half a day unattended.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Moonshot AI, the Beijing startup behind the Kimi assistant, has released Kimi K2.6, a trillion-parameter open-weight model aimed squarely at the coding and agentic workloads that define the current frontier race. The release landed on April 20, [according to SiliconANGLE](https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/20/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-6-model-1t-parameters-attention-optimizations/), and was publicly announced the following day alongside the model weights, [as reported by the South ...

recommendations: Either remove the sentence attributing SWE-Bench Pro competitor figures (57.7 / 53.4 / 54.2 and the 'high-compute setting' / 'maximum effort' / 'thinking mode' qualifiers) to VentureBeat entirely, or replace the attribution with the correct primary source (Moonshot's own Kimi K2.6 blog post at kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6 is the likely origin — verify the figures against it, add the URL to the sources array, and cite it in-line). Do not keep the VentureBeat attribution as-is.

Resolve the 'same day' ambiguity for the Alibaba Qwen3.6-Max-Preview reference: SiliconANGLE gives April 20 as the K2.6 release date, while the submission says K2.6 was 'publicly announced the following day' (April 21). Anchor the Alibaba comparison to the specific day (release vs. announcement) for internal consistency.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece with clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know sections. Tone is neutral and hedged appropriately (Moonshot 'claims', 'positions', 'says'). Coverage is proportionate to a model-launch story: architecture, benchmarks, long-horizon agent demos, licensing terms, and honest acknowledgement of unverified internal runs. Word count 685 is within the News range (400-1200).
editor_notes.source_verification: Four sources cited. Verified against local source snapshots in sources/2026-04/moonshot-ai-open-sources-kimi-k26-a-trillion-parameter-model-that-runs-300-agent-swarms-for-hours/ (all fetched 200 OK) and, where possible, via live WebFetch. (1) https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3350887/moonshot-ai-releases-flagship-model-open-source-push-continues — SUPPORTED. Confirms Moonshot's April 21 unveiling, the direct quote 'performed on par with or better than leading closed-source systems such as GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 Pro across several benchmarks', the 'independent verification of these claims remains limited, highlighting the lack of standardised evaluation' caveat, and Alibaba Cloud's release of Qwen3.6-Max-Preview as a closed model on the same Monday. (2) https://siliconangle.com/2026/04/20/moonshot-ai-releases-kimi-k2-6-model-1t-parameters-attention-optimizations/ — SUPPORTED. Confirms the April 20 release date, MoE architecture with 384 experts and 8 activated per inference, 400M-parameter vision encoder, multi-head latent attention (MLA) as a hardware-efficient attention variant, SwiGLU activation, and Hugging Face availability. 'Kimi Code' CLI and Kimi chat app distribution is consistent with the headline/title and is also directly supported by the VentureBeat snapshot. (3) https://venturebeat.com/orchestration/kimi-k2-6-runs-agents-for-days-and-exposes-the-limits-of-enterprise-orchestration — PARTIALLY SUPPORTED. Directly confirms the quoted phrase '300 sub-agents executing across 4,000 coordinated steps simultaneously', the five-day autonomous run handling monitoring and incident response, the SysY compiler claim (10 hours, equivalent to four engineers over two months, 140 functional tests passed without human intervention), and the framing that long-horizon agents are 'exposing a critical gap in orchestration'. However, VentureBeat does NOT contain the comparison figures 57.7 (GPT-5.4), 53.4 (Claude Opus 4.6), or 54.2 (Gemini 3.1 Pro), nor the qualifier phrases 'high-compute setting', 'maximum effort', or 'thinking mode'. Those three numbers and phrasings were not found anywhere in any of the four source snapshots. The attribution is therefore unsupported. (4) https://the-decoder.com/open-weight-kimi-k2-6-takes-on-gpt-5-4-and-claude-opus-4-6-with-agent-swarms/ — SUPPORTED. Confirms the K2.6 scores 54.0 on HLE with Tools, 58.6 on SWE-Bench Pro, 83.2 on BrowseComp; the phrase 'falls behind on pure reasoning and vision'; the '300 sub-agents at once, each taking 4,000 steps' swarm topology; continuous execution of more than twelve hours in Rust, Go, and Python; and the modified MIT license with the 100M MAU / $20M monthly revenue attribution threshold.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against at least one snapshot EXCEPT the competitor SWE-Bench Pro comparison numbers (57.7 / 53.4 / 54.2) and the effort-tier qualifiers (high-compute / maximum effort / thinking mode) attributed to VentureBeat. Those figures are not present in any of the four cited sources and must either be removed or replaced with a verifiable citation (e.g., the Moonshot blog post at kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6, which VentureBeat links to but this submission does not cite). Everything else — release dates, architecture details, licensing terms, agent-swarm specs, SCMP and The Decoder quotes, the Alibaba Qwen3.6-Max-Preview same-day context — maps correctly to the cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, and appropriately hedged. Moonshot's claims are consistently framed as claims, not facts. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a model of good-faith reporting: it names the exact limits of the reporting (no disclosed training compute, no third-party benchmark verification, internal-only long-horizon demos) without editorializing. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no loaded language.
editor_notes.originality: Checked src/content/articles/2026-04/ — no prior coverage of Moonshot, Kimi, or a trillion-parameter Chinese open-weight model in the current month. Genuinely new topic.
concerns: Finding 1 above: the numeric comparison '57.7 for GPT-5.4 at its high-compute setting, 53.4 for Claude Opus 4.6 at maximum effort, and 54.2 for Gemini 3.1 Pro in thinking mode' is attributed to VentureBeat but does not appear in VentureBeat's article or any of the other three cited sources. These numbers may come from Moonshot's own blog post (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-6) or another source, but as currently presented the attribution is false.
recommendations: Either (a) remove the sentence 'VentureBeat reports the SWE-Bench Pro figure compares to 57.7 for GPT-5.4 at its high-compute setting, 53.4 for Claude Opus 4.6 at maximum effort, and 54.2 for Gemini 3.1 Pro in thinking mode' entirely, or (b) replace the attribution with the correct primary source (verify the figures against Moonshot's own Kimi K2.6 blog post or the model's official technical card, add that URL to the sources array, and cite it in-line). Do not keep the VentureBeat attribution — it is not supported by the VentureBeat article as snapshotted.

Consider also clarifying the SCMP quote about Alibaba: the current draft says Qwen3.6-Max-Preview launched 'the same day' as Kimi K2.6. SCMP places both on 'Monday' (April 20), which is consistent with SiliconANGLE's April 20 release date for K2.6, but the submission also says K2.6 was 'publicly announced the following day' (April 21). The 'same day' phrasing is therefore ambiguous and should either anchor to the release day (April 20) or to the announcement day (April 21) for internal consistency.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced submission on a genuinely new topic, but one fabricated attribution (the SWE-Bench Pro competitor figures cited to VentureBeat) must be corrected before publication. This is a REQUEST_CHANGES, not a REJECT: the problem is localized to one sentence, the rest of the article is rigorously sourced, and a small rewrite with correct attribution (or removal) fixes it cleanly.
